exemplar.title: Does acquiring knowledge destroy our sense of wonder? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: Does acquiring knowledge destroy our sense of wonder? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

strengths: Maintains a sustained, clear focus on the prescribed title and explicitly structures the discussion around Natural Sciences and the Arts.
Uses concrete, relevant examples (e.g., Van Goghâs Starry Night, rainbow refraction) to support knowledge claims.
Demonstrates awareness of alternative perspectives (e.g., science can inspire awe, overanalysis in art) even if only briefly.
Presents a coherent narrative of how wonder evolves from childhood curiosity to more informed appreciation in adulthood.
weaknesses: Links to areas of knowledge are occasionally superficial and overgeneralized, limiting critical depth.
Key TOK terminology (e.g., âareas of knowledge,â âways of knowing,â definitions of âwonderâ) is not defined, weakening conceptual grounding.
Transitions between sections (Arts to Sciences, between examples) are abrupt and undermine overall cohesion.
Several examples (e.g., The Dying Gaul, childhood wonder examples) are underdeveloped or inaccurately presented, reducing credibility.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Based on the strand assessment, the essay clearly meets the 5â6 band descriptor: it remains focused on the title, offers supported arguments, and shows some evaluation of different points of view. Strengths such as structured AOK engagement and effective examples justify awarding the full 6 marks available, while noted weaknesses (superficial links, lack of TOK terminology, coherence issues, and underdeveloped or inaccurate examples) prevent advancement to the 7â8 band. This distribution aligns with the official examinerâs total of 6 and accurately reflects the essayâs overall quality.

annotations.comment: Key TOK terms such as âareas of knowledgeâ and âways of knowingâ are not defined; consider clarifying these to ground the discussion in ToK concepts.
The transition into Natural Sciences analysis is abrupt; add a signpost or topic sentence to clearly demarcate the shift from Arts to Sciences.
This roadmap paragraph outlines the argument structure but is overly repetitive; to strengthen coherence, streamline phrasing and explicitly highlight the knowledge question within each AOK.
The claim that art-making âbuilds wonderâ lacks concrete examples; integrate a specific artistic work to illustrate how emotional engagement preserves wonder.
This section correctly emphasizes the interpretive and emotional dimensions of the Arts, providing a solid foundation for later detailed examples.
The analysis would benefit from distinguishing personal and shared knowledge to explain why interpretation âdigs deeperâ and enhances wonder.
The historical claim that impasto was developed by Van Gogh is inaccurate; impasto predates himâverify historical details to maintain credibility.
The student effectively sets up the evolution of wonder from childhood to adulthood, linking innocence and curiosity to knowledge acquisition in a coherent narrative.

While evocative, the childhood examples (tooth fairy, Santa) remain descriptive and are not explicitly tied to any AOK or Way of Knowing; consider aligning early examples to introduce Natural Sciences or Arts frameworks.
This rhetorical question about mystery is engaging but the essay does not define âknowledgeâ or âwonderâ within ToK terminology; include precise definitions to sharpen the knowledge question.
The transformation of wonder from a simple âwowâ into deeper appreciation demonstrates nuanced understanding of how contextual knowledge alters perception.
The contrast between photorealism and abstraction effectively illustrates how technical mastery can either suppress or inspire wonder in visual arts.
The Vincent van Gogh example is richly detailed, providing clear context on technique and biographical influences to support the argument that knowledge can enhance wonder.
The paragraph on âThe Dying Gaulâ ends abruptly and lacks full development; ensure sentences are complete and the exampleâs relevance to the thesis is clearly articulated.
Introduce clearer transitions when shifting between artworks to maintain cohesion and reinforce how each example supports the overarching claim.
The rainbow example explains refraction accurately but lacks reflection on why scientific understanding might cultivate new forms of wonder; include this duality for balance.
Incorporate specific Ways of Knowing (e.g., perception, imagination) to deepen the exploration of how scientific explanations affect wonder.
Acknowledging that science can also inspire awe (e.g., cosmology, space exploration) would strengthen the counter-perspective beyond the brief DNA mention later.
The brain and consciousness example illustrates reductionism well but should reference relevant Ways of Knowing (e.g., reason, emotion) to anchor it in ToK frameworks.
The concluding sentence is incomplete (âprovidingâ); ensure the conclusion fully articulates the final claim on how science impacts wonder.
Consider concluding with a reflective question or broader implication for knowledge seekers to elevate the essayâs critical engagement with the prescribed title.
The shift to DNAâs wonder is a strong counterexample, but could be strengthened by integrating ethical or societal implications to enrich the evaluation.
The claim that science ârarely brings moments of aweâ is overgeneralized; consider citing specific scientific discoveries that do evoke wonder to nuance this point.
The statement that science âserves only to lessen wonderâ lacks substantiation; integrate empirical or historical examples where scientific knowledge provoked awe.
The final conclusion restates the thesis but does not synthesize evidence or reflect on implications; deepen the summation by evaluating strengths and limitations of both AOKs.

The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
